tax: Placed at family rank, Ignis-Kin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a bipedal vent-pressure flame body with a persistent humanoid form and char-ring footprint, not a fuel-ball or a creeping smoulder. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a bipedal flame body with char-ring footprint and a combustion-colour field sign.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a flame humanoid ranging 1.2–6 m with a bright torso-core. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — combustion colour (the field sign key), char-ring size and depth at each footstep, and ceiling-blackening span for the major form — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Ignis-Kin occupies vent passages, vent jets, convergences, and primary vent zones throughout the lower tiers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where vent-fault pressure ignites a humanoid combustion form and recur with the vent re-pressurises and the bipedal form re-ignites, sustaining themselves on vent-fault fuel.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: all Ignis-Kin are territorial, hold a vent zone, and track approach by radiant heat; they differ in size, colour, speed, and threat display. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: bipedal flame body, combustion-colour and char-ring field sign, vent dependence, and a form-specific suppression failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: ignition, passage-filling, fuel-draw repair, and low-flame disguise. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis-Kin matters because accounts for most formally recorded Throat fatalities in vent passages. It lets survey reports sort humanoid flame hazards by combustion colour and char-ring pattern without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Ignis-Kin is the family-rank grouping of roughly humanoid vent-pressure flame elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a bipedal body of combustion held by vent-fault pressure into a recognisably humanoid form with a bright torso-core and char-ring footprint, and divide below into genus groups by vent pressure type and resulting combustion colour — narrow (blue-white minor), single (orange-yellow standard), convergent (deep red-orange major), deep primary (three-layer nobilis). The Ignis-Kin family formalises the classic 'walking fire person' of grey-folk tradition within the Pyric order, separated from the Flameling and Cinderling by persistent humanoid form and the char-ring field sign.
